title: Memori
oneLiner: Open-source memory engine for LLM apps and agents that need persistent context injection.
summary: Memori is an open-source memory engine from GibsonAI for giving LLM applications and agents persistent memory, context injection, and configurable recall behavior.
whyItMatters: Memori matters because many agent products need a practical memory engine before they need a full agent framework. It gives teams a focused way to add durable context to conversations and workflows.
bestFor: Developers adding memory to LLM applications
Teams that want a Python-friendly memory engine
Builders comparing Mem0, Letta, and lighter memory libraries
notFor: Users who want a fully managed consumer product with no setup work
Teams that cannot review the linked source, license, and operational requirements before adoption
seoTitle: Memori: Memory Systems resource for open AI builders
seoDescription: Memori profile on OpenAgent.bot: what it is, when to use it, official links, open-source status, and structured resource data.
shareDescription: Open-source memory engine for LLM apps and agents that need persistent context injection.

coreStrengths.title: Focused memory engine
coreStrengths.description: Memori centers on memory behavior rather than broad workflow orchestration.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: A focused engine is easier to embed into existing applications.

coreStrengths.title: Persistent context injection
coreStrengths.description: The docs describe memory concepts for injecting relevant context into interactions.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Agents become more useful when recall happens at the right moment.

coreStrengths.title: Apache-2.0 open-source release
coreStrengths.description: Public materials describe Memori as Apache-2.0 open source.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Permissive licensing helps teams experiment without early legal friction.

useCaseNotes.title: Conversational memory
useCaseNotes.description: Remember preferences and prior facts across user conversations.

useCaseNotes.title: Agent task context
useCaseNotes.description: Inject previous task details when an agent resumes work.

useCaseNotes.title: Memory library evaluation
useCaseNotes.description: Compare a focused memory engine against heavier agent platforms.

compareNotes.title: Memori is a lighter memory layer
compareNotes.summary: Use Memori when you want memory inside an existing app rather than adopting a whole agent runtime.

commandLine.label: Clone Memori
commandLine.description: Use the official docs to confirm the current Python package and configuration before production use.
